description_long: This is a regional passenger airplane with a sleek, elongated fuselage painted primarily white, featuring the 'HOP!' airline branding in bold red letters on the forward section. The aircraft is equipped with two large black turboprop engines mounted on a high, straight wing configuration, each with four-bladed propellers. The tail section displays a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer, accented by a dark blue and orange livery. Numerous passenger windows line the fuselage, and the nose is rounded with cockpit windows clearly visible. The landing gear is not visible in these views, suggesting it may be retractable. The overall design is streamlined for efficient short-haul urban and regional flights.
description: A white regional passenger airplane with 'HOP!' branding, high straight wings with twin turboprop engines, and a conventional tail with blue and orange accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, 'HOP!' branding, high-mounted wings, and detailed tail livery.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the straight, high-mounted wings, twin turboprop engines, and the narrow fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar features to view 0, showing the other side of the tail and engine details.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail's vertical stabilizer, the full wingspan, and the propellers' alignment.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable tricycle configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows.
